commit.message:
refactor: inline moduledocs, remove companion .md files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite reverse order toggle to be server-side (correct DB ordering)

Previously the toggle just reversed DOM elements client-side, which broke
pagination. Now:
- Model for_feed() accepts an order param ("asc"/"desc"), uses SQL ASC/DESC
- Route reads ?order=asc and passes it through; load-more URL carries order
- Checkbox checked state is server-rendered (order == "asc")
- htmx:configRequest auto-injects the saved localStorage preference for
  any /episodes?feed_id= request, so navigation from feeds list and
  load-more both get the right order without explicit URL changes
- setReverseOrder() saves preference then triggers an HTMX reload of
  #content with the correct order param

commit.message:
feat(catalog): filter versioning & grouped catalog (#265)

## Summary

- **DB migration**: adds `introduced_at`, `deprecated_at`,
`successor_hash` columns to `filters` table for version tracking
- **Grouped catalog**: new `catalog/grouped.json` R2 artifact that
merges filters by normalized command pattern (e.g. `gh issue view *` and
`gh issue view` are grouped together), with primary filter selection
(current stdlib preferred)
- **New endpoints**: `GET /api/catalog/grouped` (bearer auth) and `POST
/api/filters/backfill-versions` (service auth)
- **Publish-stdlib enhancements**: tracks `introduced_at` version, runs
deprecation sweep to mark removed/replaced stdlib filters
- **Backfill CLI**: `tokf backfill-versions` command reconstructs
version history from git tags (gated behind `stdlib-publish` feature)
- **CI workflow**: `backfill-versions.yml` (workflow_dispatch) for
one-shot historical backfill

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for grouping logic, primary selection, wildcard
normalization, serde round-trips
- [x] Unit tests for version info serialization (None fields omitted)
- [x] Unit tests for R2 write functions (in-memory storage mock)
- [x] Unit tests for backfill semver comparison and timeline computation
- [x] DB integration tests: insert filters with version data, verify
grouped catalog query
- [x] E2E: publish-stdlib with version → verify deprecation sweep
- [ ] Manual: run `tokf backfill-versions --dry-run` against repo

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
Fix UI freeze on large configs via lazy block parsing (#95)

Make block element types (BLOCK_STMT, MAP_BLOCK_STMT, etc.) lazy-parseable
using ILazyParseableElementType so BlockSupportImpl only reparses the
changed block instead of the entire file, preventing DiffTree.build
stack overflow.

- Add NginxLazyBlockElementType extending ILazyParseableElementType
- Add NginxElementTypeFactory to route block types to lazy element type
- Add NginxLazyBlockParser overriding parse_root_ for block dispatch
- Update Nginx.bnf with elementTypeFactory attribute
- Bump version to 2026.1.10

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add goals system, AI triage, orchestrator, and task execution

Add bartering system with haggling dialogue and Goblin Peddler NPC

Implements the bartering-through-dialogue user story. Adds multi-round
negotiation to Morrigan and a new Goblin Peddler NPC (Grikkle):

Morrigan haggling:
- Multi-branch negotiation for healing tonics and blade oil
- Trade currency is information (rumors, stories, Deepscript knowledge)
- Social skill checks affect deal quality (persuade/deceive/intimidate)
- Hidden "Essence of the Deep" stock unlocked at friendly mood
- Better intel = better rewards (+5 to +15 HP, +1 to +4 ATK)
- Failed threats/lies still get basic items with comedy

Grikkle the Goblin Peddler (new NPC, spawns from level 1):
- Entrepreneurial goblin merchant selling rocks and mushrooms
- Full backstory (exiled for being "un-goblin," MBA from broken book)
- Haggling via compliments, information trades, and social checks
- Señor Pebbleston (executive rock with tiny hat), Mr. Bones the
  skeleton customer, Professor Honkington callbacks
- Deep lore: discovers dungeon manufactures its own items
- "Grikkle's Five-Year Plan: Step 1: rocks. Step 2: ???. Step 3: gold"

test: add two-process netplay E2E screenshot verification (#74)

Add a headless NetplayTestRunner that launches two JVM processes (host +
client) connected via WebSocket relay, plays Chip 'n Dale in 2-player
netplay, and verifies cross-player input propagation using screenshots:

- Phase 1: P1 presses Select on title screen → cursor moves on P2's screen
- Phase 2: P2 presses A in-game (Dale jumps) → visible change on P1's screen

Key fixes to make netplay input work correctly:
- Add netplayLocalButtons field to avoid JNI feedback loop where
  applyInputToJni output was re-captured as new input by captureLocalInput
- Seed early frames with empty inputs for both ports to prevent deadlock
- Fire-and-forget button press timing to capture mid-jump screenshots
